response.justification: James is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture expertise, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his history. His closest relevant experience (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ tooling at National Instruments) is adjacent at best and does not approach the hardware architecture depth expected at principal level.
strengths: Deep software engineering experience (15+ years) meeting the BS + 8 years educational path
Low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second with async I/O and caching
Some systems-level C/C++ experience and embedded exposure (National Instruments, robotics work)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture at principal IC level

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU optimization background
Strong general software engineering with some low-latency systems work
